Loading and preprocessing the data
----------------------------------

    data <- read.csv("activity.csv")
    head(data)

    ##   steps       date interval
    ## 1    NA 2012-10-01        0
    ## 2    NA 2012-10-01        5
    ## 3    NA 2012-10-01       10
    ## 4    NA 2012-10-01       15
    ## 5    NA 2012-10-01       20
    ## 6    NA 2012-10-01       25

------------------------------------------------------------------------

What is mean total number of steps taken per day?
-------------------------------------------------

    steps_per_day <- data %>%
      group_by(date) %>%
      summarise(total_steps = sum(steps, na.rm = TRUE))

    ## `summarise()` ungrouping output (override with `.groups` argument)

    hist(steps_per_day$total_steps,
         main = "Total Steps Per Day",
         xlab = "Steps",
         col = "blue")

![](PA1_template_files/figure-markdown_strict/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png)

    mean_steps <- mean(steps_per_day$total_steps)
    median_steps <- median(steps_per_day$total_steps)

    mean_steps

    ## [1] 9354.23

    median_steps

    ## [1] 10395

The mean number of steps per day is **9354.23** and the median is
**1.039510^{4}**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

What is the average daily activity pattern?
-------------------------------------------

    avg_interval <- data %>%
      group_by(interval) %>%
      summarise(avg_steps = mean(steps, na.rm = TRUE))

    ## `summarise()` ungrouping output (override with `.groups` argument)

    plot(avg_interval$interval, avg_interval$avg_steps,
         type = "l",
         xlab = "Interval",
         ylab = "Average Steps")

![](PA1_template_files/figure-markdown_strict/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png)

    avg_interval[which.max(avg_interval$avg_steps), ]

    ## # A tibble: 1 x 2
    ##   interval avg_steps
    ##      <int>     <dbl>
    ## 1      835      206.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Imputing missing values
-----------------------

    sum(is.na(data$steps))

    ## [1] 2304

    data_filled <- data

    for(i in 1:nrow(data_filled)){
      if(is.na(data_filled$steps)){
        data_filled$steps[i] <- avg_interval$avg_steps[
          avg_interval$interval == data_filled$interval[i]
        ]
      }
    }

    ## Warning in if (is.na(data_filled$steps)) {: the condition has length > 1 and only the first element
    ## will be used

    steps_per_day2 <- data_filled %>%
      group_by(date) %>%
      summarise(total_steps = sum(steps))

    ## `summarise()` ungrouping output (override with `.groups` argument)

    hist(steps_per_day2$total_steps,
         main = "Total Steps Per Day (After Imputation)",
         xlab = "Steps",
         col = "green")

![](PA1_template_files/figure-markdown_strict/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png)

    mean2 <- mean(steps_per_day2$total_steps)
    median2 <- median(steps_per_day2$total_steps)

    mean2

    ## [1] NA

    median2

    ## [1] NA

After imputing missing values, the mean is **NA** and the median is
**NA**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Are there differences in activity patterns between weekdays and weekends?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

    data_filled$date <- as.Date(data_filled$date)

    data_filled$day_type <- ifelse(
      weekdays(data_filled$date) %in% c("Saturday", "Sunday"),
      "Weekend",
      "Weekday"
    )

    avg_daytype <- data_filled %>%
      group_by(interval, day_type) %>%
      summarise(avg_steps = mean(steps))

    ## `summarise()` regrouping output by 'interval' (override with `.groups` argument)

    ggplot(avg_daytype, aes(x = interval, y = avg_steps)) +
      geom_line() +
      facet_wrap(~day_type) +
      labs(title = "Activity Pattern: Weekday vs Weekend",
           x = "Interval",
           y = "Average Steps")

    ## Warning: Removed 576 row(s) containing missing values (geom_path).

![](PA1_template_files/figure-markdown_strict/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png)

The activity patterns differ between weekdays and weekends.
